sunlight: Low to bright indirect light; tolerates very low light
water: Every 2-6 weeks; allow soil to dry out completely between waterings
notes: One of the best air-purifying plants; nearly indestructible; great for beginners

sunlight: Low to bright indirect light; avoid direct sun
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow top inch of soil to dry
toxicity: Toxic to cats, dogs, and humans if ingested
notes: Extremely hardy and fast-growing; propagates easily in water; trailing or climbing habit

sunlight: Bright indirect light; tolerates medium light
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow top 2 inches of soil to dry
humidity: High; mist leaves or use humidifier
soil: Well-draining, rich potting mix with perlite
notes: Iconic fenestrated leaves develop with age and more light; loves a moss pole to climb

sunlight: Low to medium indirect light; avoid direct sun
water: Every 1-2 weeks; keep soil lightly moist; droops dramatically when thirsty
toxicity: Toxic to cats, dogs, and humans
notes: One of very few flowering plants that thrives in low light; excellent air purifier

sunlight: Bright indirect light; tolerates low light
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow soil to partially dry
notes: Produces 'spiderettes' (baby plants) on long runners; great air purifier; pet-safe

sunlight: Low to bright indirect light; highly adaptable
water: Every 2-3 weeks; allow soil to dry completely; extremely drought tolerant
soil: Well-draining potting mix or cactus mix
toxicity: Toxic to cats, dogs, and humans
notes: Stores water in rhizomes; nearly impossible to kill from neglect; glossy attractive leaves

sunlight: Bright indirect to some direct morning light
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow top inch to dry; sensitive to overwatering and root rot
notes: Notoriously finicky; hates being moved; needs consistent conditions; large dramatic leaves

sunlight: Bright indirect light; tolerates medium light
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow top inch to dry; reduce in winter
notes: Large glossy leaves; grows into a tree indoors; wipe leaves with damp cloth to remove dust

sunlight: Bright direct to indirect light; 6+ hours preferred
water: Every 2-3 weeks; allow to dry completely; extremely drought tolerant
soil: Sandy, fast-draining cactus or succulent mix
toxicity: Toxic to cats and dogs; gel safe for human skin topically
notes: Gel from leaves soothes burns and skin irritation; avoid overwatering at all costs

sunlight: Indirect bright light; avoid direct sun
water: Frequently; keep soil consistently moist but not waterlogged
humidity: High; mist daily or use a pebble tray with water
notes: Dramatic arching fronds; drops leaves quickly without humidity; great for bathrooms

sunlight: Bright direct to indirect light; 4+ hours of sun
water: Every 2-3 weeks; allow soil to dry completely; reduce in winter
notes: Considered a good luck plant; resembles a miniature tree; leaves store water

sunlight: Low to medium indirect light; darker varieties tolerate lower light
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow top half of soil to dry
notes: Beautiful variegated foliage; one of the most adaptable houseplants; air purifying

sunlight: Indirect light; tolerates low light
water: Every 2 weeks; allow top inch to dry; sensitive to fluoride in tap water
notes: Long narrow leaves with red edges; cane-like stems; use filtered water to prevent tip burn

sunlight: Medium to bright indirect light; tolerates low light
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow top inch to dry
toxicity: Toxic to cats, dogs, and humans
notes: Heart-shaped glossy leaves; vining habit; one of the easiest plants to propagate

sunlight: Medium to bright indirect light; no direct sun
water: Every 1-2 weeks; keep soil lightly moist; use distilled or filtered water
humidity: High; very sensitive to dry air
notes: Stunning patterned leaves that fold up at night; very sensitive to chemicals in water

sunlight: Full to partial sun; 6+ hours preferred; can tolerate bright indirect
water: Every 2-3 weeks; allow to dry completely; soak and dry method
soil: Fast-draining cactus/succulent mix with coarse sand
notes: Rosette shape; comes in stunning colors; etiolation (stretching) signals insufficient light

sunlight: Full sun; 6+ hours of direct sunlight required
water: Every 3-4 weeks in summer; monthly or less in winter; never let sit in water
soil: Very fast-draining cactus mix with gravel
notes: Extremely drought tolerant; slow growing; sharp spines; can live decades

sunlight: Bright indirect light; east or north-facing window ideal
water: Every 7-10 days; allow to dry slightly between waterings; ice cube method works
soil: Orchid bark or sphagnum moss; never regular potting soil
notes: Most popular orchid; reblooms with temperature differential; aerial roots are normal

sunlight: Full sun to bright indirect; minimum 4-6 hours direct sun
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow top 2 inches to dry; reduce in winter
toxicity: Mildly toxic to cats, dogs, and humans
notes: Large paddle-shaped leaves; dramatic tropical look; blooms after several years indoors

sunlight: Full sun; minimum 6-8 hours of direct sunlight
water: Every 1-2 weeks; drought tolerant once established; excellent drainage essential
soil: Well-draining, sandy or gritty alkaline soil
toxicity: Mildly toxic to cats and dogs
notes: Intensely fragrant; attracts pollinators; prune after flowering to maintain shape

sunlight: Full sun; 6-8 hours of direct sunlight minimum
water: Frequently; keep soil consistently moist but well-drained; water at base
notes: Pinch off flowers to extend harvest; aromatic culinary herb; wilts dramatically when thirsty

sunlight: Full sun; 6-8 hours minimum
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow soil to dry between waterings; drought tolerant
notes: Intensely aromatic culinary herb; great on a sunny windowsill; prone to root rot if overwatered

sunlight: Partial to full sun; 4-6 hours
water: Frequently; keep soil consistently moist
notes: Extremely vigorous; grows invasively if planted in ground; contain in pots; propagates easily

sunlight: Full sun; 8+ hours of direct sunlight required
water: Consistently moist; deeply every 2-3 days; mulch to retain moisture
soil: Rich, deep, well-draining potting or garden soil
toxicity: Leaves and stems toxic; fruit edible
notes: Heavy feeder; needs staking; pinch suckers for indeterminate varieties; calcium prevents blossom end rot

sunlight: Bright indirect to medium light; no direct sun
water: Every 1-2 weeks; keep evenly moist; good drainage important
notes: One of the best palms for indoor use; excellent air purifier; graceful clumping habit

sunlight: Bright indirect light; some morning direct sun acceptable
water: Every 2-3 weeks; allow to dry between waterings; very susceptible to rot
toxicity: Toxic to cats, dogs, and humans
notes: Unique trailing bead-like leaves; excellent in hanging baskets; fragile stems

sunlight: Full sun; 4-6 hours of direct sunlight required
water: Use only distilled or rainwater; keep in tray of water; never let dry out
temperature: 70-95°F (21-35°C) in growing season; needs winter dormancy below 50°F
soil: Sphagnum moss or 1:1 perlite and peat; never add fertilizer
notes: Never feed it meat; use live or recently dead insects; needs winter dormancy; tap water kills it

sunlight: Bright indirect light; north or east window; avoid direct sun
water: Every 1-2 weeks; water from bottom to avoid leaf spotting; keep soil lightly moist
soil: African violet specialty mix; loose and well-draining
notes: Blooms nearly year-round with proper light; water from bottom only; no cold water on leaves

sunlight: Low to bright indirect light; tolerates shade
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow top inch to dry
toxicity: Toxic to cats, dogs, and humans
notes: Great trailing or climbing plant; spider mites common in dry conditions; invasive outdoors

sunlight: Bright direct to indirect light; needs good light for vibrant colors
water: Every 1-2 weeks; keep evenly moist; reduce in winter
toxicity: Toxic to cats, dogs, and humans
notes: Spectacular multicolored foliage; drops leaves when stressed by cold or low light; dramatic plant

sunlight: Bright indirect light; some morning direct sun acceptable
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow top inch to dry; reduce after flowering
soil: Well-draining potting mix with added perlite
notes: Unlike desert cacti, needs more water; cool dark nights in fall trigger blooms; heirloom plant

sunlight: Full sun; 6+ hours of direct sunlight
water: Frequently; keep consistently moist in summer; reduce in winter
notes: Stunning large blooms last one day; heavy feeder during growing season; bring indoors before frost

sunlight: Bright indirect light; tolerates medium light
water: Every 2 weeks; allow to dry between waterings; store water in thick leaves
soil: Well-draining mix with perlite or orchid bark
notes: Produces clusters of fragrant star-shaped flowers; do not remove flower spurs; slow but rewarding

sunlight: Low to medium indirect light; avoid direct sun
water: Frequently; keep soil consistently moist; droops dramatically when thirsty
notes: Stunning veined foliage; great terrarium plant; faints dramatically without water but recovers quickly

sunlight: Bright indirect light; no direct sun on waxy flowers
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow top inch to dry; mist leaves
soil: Orchid or aroid mix; well-draining and airy
toxicity: Toxic to cats, dogs, and humans
notes: Waxy heart-shaped spathes in red, pink, or white; blooms year-round with proper light

sunlight: Full sun; 6+ hours of direct sunlight
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow soil to dry between waterings; good drainage essential
lifespan: Tender perennial; often grown as annual
notes: Abundant blooms in many colors; deadhead spent flowers; fragrant foliage

sunlight: Bright indirect light; no direct sun
water: Frequently; keep soil consistently moist; very sensitive to drying out
humidity: Very high; difficult to maintain indoors without extra humidity
notes: Delicate fan-shaped leaflets on black stems; most challenging fern; thrives in bathrooms

sunlight: Full sun to bright indirect; very tolerant
water: Every 2-3 weeks; allow to dry completely; extremely drought tolerant
notes: Nearly indestructible; sharp leaf tips; dramatic architectural plant; rarely needs repotting

sunlight: Full sun; 8+ hours of direct sunlight; south-facing window ideal
water: Every 1-2 weeks; keep evenly moist; reduce in winter; excellent drainage
notes: Fragrant flowers; hand pollinate indoors; feed with citrus fertilizer monthly; bring outside in summer

sunlight: Bright indirect light; tolerates medium light
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow top inch to dry
notes: Glossy palmate leaves; fast-growing; wipe leaves occasionally; tolerates some neglect

sunlight: Bright indirect light; avoid harsh direct sun
water: Fill center cup with water; change weekly; keep soil lightly moist
lifespan: Monocarpic; dies after blooming but produces pups
notes: Dramatic central flower spike; parent plant dies after blooming; propagate pups (offsets)

sunlight: Bright indirect to some direct morning light
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow to dry slightly; goes dormant in summer heat
toxicity: Toxic to cats and dogs in large quantities
notes: Folds leaves at night and reopens in morning; goes dormant in summer; regrows from bulbs

sunlight: Bright indirect to medium light; needs light for purple color
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow top inch to dry
toxicity: Mildly toxic; can cause skin irritation
notes: Striking purple and silver striped leaves; extremely fast-growing; propagates instantly in water

sunlight: Full sun; 6+ hours of direct sunlight
water: Frequently; keep consistently moist; does not tolerate drying out
lifespan: Perennial rhizome; annual in cold climates
notes: Bold tropical foliage and dramatic flowers; store rhizomes indoors over winter in cold climates

sunlight: Medium to bright indirect light
water: Every 1-2 weeks; allow top inch to dry; fairly drought tolerant
notes: Trailing habit; ideal for hanging baskets; scalloped leaves; grows rapidly

sunlight: Very low light; tolerates near-darkness; avoid direct sun
water: Every 2-3 weeks; very drought tolerant; allow to dry
notes: Earns its name; virtually indestructible; tolerates neglect, dust, low light, temperature swings

sunlight: Full sun; 6+ hours
water: Aquatic; floats on water surface
soil: No soil needed; roots dangle in water
notes: Beautiful purple flowers; excellent water purifier; invasive in warm climates outdoors
